mission: ARCH: Design PG migration strategy to close the SQLite→PG gap

verification_of_inv004.verdict: NO GAP — SQLite is intentionally fully removed; 4 PG migrations cover all 19 tables across 10 .pg.sql.ts schema files
verification_of_inv004.pg_baseline_detail: The 20260529 initial schema dump (yellow_molly_hayes) was a deliberate clean-slate squash of all previous SQLite migrations into a single PG baseline. This is correct and intentional.
evidence: packages/opencode/migration/ directory does not exist (deleted May 30)
Only drizzle.config.pg.ts exists (no SQLite drizzle config)
Zero imports from drizzle-orm/sqlite in src/
package.json #db alias resolves to db.pg.ts for all targets
applyMigrations() IS called at startup (adapter.ts:194) — prior INV-003 finding was stale
Migration tests exist and pass (migration.pg.test.ts, migrate.test.ts)

current_state.dual_backend: PGlite (embedded, for :memory: / file paths) and node-postgres (remote PG, for connection strings)
current_state.idempotency: None — runs all SQL unconditionally on every startup. No __drizzle_migrations tracking.

migration_philosophy.baseline_strategy: Squash baseline: initial migration (20260529) is a complete schema dump. All net-new schema changes go into sequential PG migrations.
migration_philosophy.naming_convention: Drizzle Kit auto-generates: YYYYMMDDHHmmss_snake_case_name. Keep this — it's the Drizzle standard.
migration_philosophy.idempotency_principle: applyMigrations() is called at every startup. Drizzle's migrator for node-postgres tracks applied migrations via __drizzle_migrations table. PGlite path has no such tracking — this is acceptable for local/memory databases but should be documented.
migration_philosophy.schema_source_of_truth: drizzle.config.pg.ts scans ./src/**/*.pg.sql.ts. All schema changes start by editing the .pg.sql.ts files, then running db:generate:pg to produce the migration.
migration_philosophy.going_forward: Every schema change: edit the .pg.sql.ts file → run `bun run db:generate:pg` → commit the generated migration-pg/*/migration.sql. The old SQLite migration/ directory is gone and will never return.

root_causes.hypothesis: SQLite migration directory was deleted May 30 but build.ts still references it — latent build failure
evidence: build.ts:21 reads path.join(dir, 'migration') — directory confirmed absent
build.ts:220 embeds OPENCODE_MIGRATIONS define with dead data
build-node.ts correctly references migration-pg/ instead (shows the fix pattern)

root_causes.hypothesis: SQLite terminology persists in adapter code despite Postgres-only reality
evidence: db.ts:25: skipMigrations flag declared but never wired

root_causes.hypothesis: applyMigrations() has no idempotency guard for PGlite path
evidence: db.pg.ts:74-82: PGlite path reads all migration files and exec()s them unconditionally
node-postgres path auto-tracks via drizzle's migrator

fixes.title: Fix dead migration-loading code in build.ts
fixes.description: build.ts:21-46 reads from the deleted migration/ directory. Replace with migration-pg/ path, matching the pattern in build-node.ts.

after.line_220: (unchanged — migrations variable now populated from migration-pg/ instead of migration/)

tests_to_run: No src tests affected — this is build-only. Build smoke test: verify binary builds with OPENCODE_MIGRATIONS populated.

risks: LOW: If build.ts is not currently run (builds use build-node.ts or a different path), this is a no-op cleanup
LOW: The migrations variable is embedded as a build-time define but never used at runtime in src/ — removal is safe
validation: Verify build.ts compiles after change (bun build check)
Verify OPENCODE_MIGRATIONS define still works if referenced at runtime
fixes.alternate_approach: Remove lines 21-46 and line 220 entirely if OPENCODE_MIGRATIONS is truly unused at runtime. Search runtime source shows zero references — safe to remove.

fixes.title: Wire skipMigrations flag from RuntimeFlags into adapter
fixes.description: The skipMigrations flag is declared in RuntimeFlags (env: OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS) and read in db.ts:25's DatabaseFlags type, but never passed to makePgAdapter() or makeLocalPgAdapter(). Wire it so that setting OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS=true actually skips migrations at startup.

after.db.ts_DatabaseFlags_passthrough: In Client() constructor, read skipMigrations from flags and pass it as an option when constructing the adapter. OR: remove skipMigrations from DatabaseFlags and add it directly to makePgAdapter's options interface.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/storage/adapter-dual-write.ts (pass through to both makeLocalPgAdapter and makePgAdapter)

risks: MEDIUM: Changing makePgAdapter signature may break callers. Search all callers before refactoring.
LOW: The flag already defaults to false — adding the check is purely additive
LOW: adapter-dual-write.ts constructs both adapters — must pass the flag through
validation: Start with OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS=true → verify no migration logs appear
Start normally (flag unset) → verify migrations still run
Run migration.pg.test.ts → should still pass (no flag set in tests)
Run adapter.unit.test.ts → verify no signature breakage
fixes.alternate_approach: Remove skipMigrations from DatabaseFlags and db.ts entirely since it's unused. Simpler, zero risk. Re-add only when a concrete use case exists.

fixes.title: Rename sqlite→local in adapter-dual-write.ts
fixes.description: Variable names sqliteAdapter, sqliteDb, sqliteTx mislead — they wrap PGlite (local embedded PG), not SQLite. Rename to localAdapter, localDb, localTx.

tests_to_run: test/storage/adapter.unit.test.ts (indirect — adapter-dual-write is tested through the Layer)

risks: LOW: Mechanical rename, no public API change — makeDualWriteAdapter parameter names are internal
LOW: No other files reference these parameter names
validation: Verify no remaining 'sqlite' string in adapter-dual-write.ts except in comments/doc

fixes.title: Rename SQLiteTransactionOptions → LocalTransactionOptions in adapter.ts
fixes.description: The SQLiteTransactionOptions type with _tag: 'sqlite' is used for PGlite transactions. Rename to LocalTransactionOptions with _tag: 'pglite' to reflect reality.
risks: MEDIUM: Type is exported and part of TransactionOptions union — any consumer that pattern-matches on _tag === 'sqlite' will break
LOW: Search shows only adapter.ts:125 and adapter.unit.test.ts:86 reference the _tag
CONSIDER: Add a backward-compat alias: type SQLiteTransactionOptions = LocalTransactionOptions for one release cycle
validation: Search entire codebase for _tag: 'sqlite' and _tag === 'sqlite' — only 2 matches found (both fixed above)
Run adapter.unit.test.ts → test 'accepts SQLite transaction options' should pass with renamed type

fixes.title: Document the migration philosophy in AGENTS.md
fixes.description: Add a clear migration philosophy section to packages/opencode/AGENTS.md documenting: squash baseline strategy, naming conventions, how to add new migrations, idempotency behavior, and the dual-backend (PGlite vs node-postgres) migration path.

before.existing_line: - **Migrations**: generated by Drizzle Kit using `drizzle.config.pg.ts` (schema: `./src/**/*.pg.sql.ts`, output: `./migration-pg`).

after.new_section:
- **Migrations**: generated by Drizzle Kit using `drizzle.config.pg.ts` (schema: `./src/**/*.pg.sql.ts`, output: `./migration-pg`).
- **Migration philosophy**: 
  - The first migration (`20260529_*`) is a squash baseline — a complete schema dump that replaces all prior SQLite migrations.
  - All subsequent migrations are sequential, additive PG migrations generated by Drizzle Kit.
  - To add a migration: edit the `.pg.sql.ts` schema file → run `bun run db:generate:pg` → commit the generated SQL.
  - `applyMigrations()` runs at every startup (fire-and-forget in `makePgAdapter()`).
  - The node-postgres path tracks applied migrations via `__drizzle_migrations`; the PGlite path re-runs all SQL unconditionally (acceptable for local/memory DBs).
  - Migration naming follows Drizzle Kit's auto-generated format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss_snake_case_name`.
  - To skip migrations at startup: set `OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS=true`.
  - The old SQLite `migration/` directory is permanently removed. Do not recreate it.

validation: Verify conventions match actual drizzle.config.pg.ts settings

fixes.title: Rename _SQLiteSatisfiesDLC type alias in adapter.ts
fixes.description: The compile-time type assertion _SQLiteSatisfiesDLC at adapter.ts:409 has a misleading name and stale comment about SQLite. Rename to _PgSatisfiesDLC or remove if unused.

before.line_409: type _SQLiteSatisfiesDLC = DrizzleLikeClient extends any ? true : never // structural: SQLite client satisfies via duck typing

after.line_409: type _PgSatisfiesDLC = DrizzleLikeClient extends any ? true : never // structural: PG client satisfies via duck typing

risks: NONE: This is a compile-time-only type alias, never exported or consumed at runtime
validation: Run bun typecheck — type alias should still compile

plan.apply_order_rationale: Documentation first (no code risk) → dead build code (no runtime risk) → skipMigrations wiring (behavioral change) → mechanical renames (low risk, high clarity) → type renames (need test update) → final alias cleanup

deferred_to_future.title: Add PGlite idempotency guard (__drizzle_migrations tracking)
deferred_to_future.reason: The node-postgres path auto-tracks via drizzle's migrator, but the PGlite path re-runs all SQL files unconditionally. This is acceptable for local/memory databases where startup is fast and migrations are additive, but for file-backed PGlite with many migrations, this adds unnecessary work.
deferred_to_future.when: When migration count exceeds 10-15 OR when file-backed PGlite startup becomes slow

deferred_to_future.title: Rename opencode.db to opencode.pg
deferred_to_future.reason: db.ts:40 getChannelPath returns 'opencode.db' — this is a legacy SQLite filename convention. The content is PGlite. Low priority because .db is just a file extension convention.
deferred_to_future.when: When the next major version introduces breaking changes OR when user confusion is reported

not_worth_fixing.description: File extension filter Set includes 'sqlite' as a binary file extension. This is a file-type classifier, not a database dependency. 'sqlite' files remain valid binary files — this is correct.
not_worth_fixing.reason: Not SQLite code — just a file extension name in a file-type classifier.

out_of_scope_findings_recorded: INV-004 recorded 6 findings in .build/rig-relay/pg_migration_gap.v1.json — all addressed by this plan
INV-003 had a stale finding (applyMigrations never called) — corrected by INV-004 evidence

rollback_plan: Revert each fix in reverse order: fix-F → fix-D → fix-C → fix-B → fix-A → fix-E. All changes are independent — no fix depends on another fix being applied first.

validation_strategy.step_1_typecheck: bun typecheck in packages/opencode after all fixes
validation_strategy.step_3_full_suite: bun test in packages/opencode (storage tests at minimum)
validation_strategy.step_4_build_smoke: Verify packages/opencode/script/build.ts compiles without runtime errors (the migration-pg/ path resolves)
validation_strategy.step_5_grep_cleanup: rg 'sqlite' packages/opencode/src/storage/ — should only match in comments, the file classifier, or the nofix-1 exception
